nuggets.headline: From Bogota to Fairfax: Pete Rango's Early Musical Escape
nuggets.text: Pete Rango's musical journey began when he was just nine years old, teaching himself guitar in Bogota, Colombia, after a clash with his teacher. Two years later, his family moved to Fairfax, Virginia, seeking refuge from guerrilla persecution. It was this early DIY spirit that blossomed as he formed a metal/hardcore band in high school, which pushed him to learn recording and production on his own.
nuggets.imageCaption: Pete Rango, a multi-instrumentalist, playing bass in the studio.

nuggets.headline: Disrupting the System with 'Savage Planet' Sound
nuggets.text: You're listening to "Oms at Play" from Pete Rango's 2022 album, *Savage Planet*. This album, which also features tracks like "Planet X" and "Traag Park" with Xmane, directly reflects Pete's core philosophy: to disrupt, uplift, and challenge the system. As an artist, producer, and culture-builder, Pete crafts entire sonic worlds where community, creativity, and technology come together, all powered by his signature DIY spirit.
nuggets.imageCaption: Oms at Play by Pete Rango

nuggets.headline: If 'Oms at Play' grooves, explore FKJ's eclectic sound
nuggets.text: If you're digging the inventive blend of sounds in "Oms at Play," you've got to check out FKJ, also known as French Kiwi Juice. He's a multi-instrumentalist and producer who masterfully weaves live instrumentation with electronic beats, creating a smooth, soulful, and often improvisational vibe that feels genuinely fresh. His music often builds similar intricate, genre-bending worlds that Pete Rango creates.
nuggets.imageCaption: FKJ, a multi-instrumentalist known for his soulful electronic productions.

nuggets.headline: Pete Rango's College Expulsion: Kicked Out by Dad Despite Straight A/B Grades
nuggets.text: Even a straight A/B average couldn't save Pete Rango from a challenging moment during his first semester at VCU in 2008. After discovering electronic music and getting into mashups, he was abruptly kicked out of his home by his dad for smoking pot, leading him to move with his mom in Fort Myers, Florida.
nuggets.imageCaption: Pete Rango in the studio, playing a bass guitar.

nuggets.headline: Before HBO Placements, Pete Rango Led a Metal/Hardcore Band
nuggets.text: Long before producing hits for HBO's 'Insecure' or developing artists, Pete Rango cut his teeth in the high school metal/hardcore scene with his band, In Alcatraz 1962. This intense experience was the true catalyst, pushing him to teach himself the ins and outs of recording, mixing, and producing his own music.
nuggets.imageCaption: Oms at Play by Pete Rango

nuggets.headline: If 'Oms at Play' grooves, you'll love Tom Misch's laid-back funk
nuggets.text: If the current vibe of Pete Rango’s 'Oms at Play' is hitting just right, then you absolutely need to explore the world of London-based multi-instrumentalist Tom Misch. His signature blend of soulful R&B, jazz, and hip-hop beats creates an equally smooth and intricate sound that’s perfect for those deep listening moments.
nuggets.imageCaption: Tom Misch performing his distinctive blend of soulful music.

nuggets.headline: At Nine, Pete Rango Taught Himself Guitar Due to a Tricky Teacher
nuggets.text: Pete Rango's deep musical commitment started incredibly early, picking up the guitar at just nine years old. Interestingly, he actually taught himself to play because he just couldn't quite gel with his appointed guitar teacher. That early, self-reliant approach clearly laid the groundwork for the powerful DIY spirit he embodies today, pushing him to build his own creative path as an artist and producer.
nuggets.imageCaption: Pete Rango in the studio, continuing his musical journey.

nuggets.headline: From Metal Bands to Live Audio for ASAP Ferg, Pete Rango's Expansive Journey
nuggets.text: As 'Oms at Play' sets its vibe, think about Pete Rango's incredible musical journey and how it shapes his sound. He went from fronting a metal/hardcore band in high school, to producing electronic and hip-hop, and even spent years freelancing as a live audio engineer for major artists like Dom Kennedy and ASAP Ferg. This broad experience—from raw punk energy to intricate electronic production and managing live sound for huge concerts—all feeds into the unique, genre-colliding musical worlds he meticulously crafts in the studio.
nuggets.imageCaption: for major artists like Dom Kennedy and ASAP Ferg

nuggets.headline: If 'Oms at Play' has you grooving, check out SG Lewis's refined electronica
nuggets.text: If Pete Rango's blend of electronic and smooth rhythms on 'Oms at Play' is hitting just right, then you absolutely need to dive into SG Lewis. He's a master at crafting lush, dance-ready tracks that often feature incredible vocalists, perfectly fusing disco, house, and R&B elements into a sound that feels both classic and fresh. Start with his album *times* or his EPs like *Dark*.
nuggets.imageCaption: SG Lewis blends house, disco, and R&B.
